spans.text:
Attendance
Daily classroom attendance is required: Daily attendance and participation is the key to
developing your foreign language proficiency and competence.
• Please come to the class on time. Coming late, into a class in progress, will not only affect
you, but also distract the whole class.
• If you come late for the class, points will be taken from your daily attendance. A student
who comes in 15 minutes late will be considered absent.
spans.label: ATTEND

spans.text:
• Grading policy for Attendance and daily participation
o 0 points: absent or come late more than 15 minutes.
o 1 point: attended, but did not actively participate or showed lack of preparation, or
came late more than 10 minutes.
o 2 points: attended and actively participated and showed preparation
spans.label: GRADE

spans.text: • More than 13 absences will result in automatic failure of the course.\n• Please contact the instructor in advance about any unavoidable absence or provide\nevidence (e.g. a note from an advisor, a doctor, or an appropriate office).
spans.label: ATTEND

spans.text:
• Attending a section in which you are not enrolled is not permitted. Only those who
submitted “Time Conflict Form” can attend another section. In cases of “excused” absence,
you may attend another section. If the absence is NOT "excused," you will not get an
attendance/participation credit by attending another section. Still, it may be better to attend
another section than not attending at all, so that you do not miss the lesson content.
• Unexcused absences will result in a zero point for your daily participation. You are
responsible for checking the material covered during your missed class.
spans.label: ATTEND

spans.text:
Examples of Excused Absences:
Participation in athletics / Participation in required student activities / Illness and personal
crisis / Religious observances / Jury duty, etc. Please consult your instructor if you have
questions.
spans.label: ATTEND

spans.text: Assignments\nAssignments must be turned in before the class on each due date.
spans.label: ASSIGN

spans.text:
• Points (10% every 24 hours) will be taken off for lateness and poor work.
• No credit is given when an assignment is submitted more than 3 days late (more than 72
hours late).
• Assignments turned in late due to excused absences must be accompanied by a doctor’s
note and/or other evidence.
spans.label: LATE

spans.text: • It is all right to discuss homework with your classmates. However, it is your responsibility\nto write your own individual answers. Copying or letting someone else copy an assignment\nis “cheating.”
spans.label: INTEGRITY

spans.text: Please check Moodle for due dates and contact your instructor or course assistants if you have\nproblems with your homework.
spans.label: ADMIN

spans.text: Exams and Quizzes\nThere are two quizzes (vocabulary and Kanji) and one exam per one chapter.
spans.label: ASSIGN

spans.text:
You are allowed to make up quizzes and exams you missed within ONE week from the scheduled
date/time. Each make-up quiz/exam is worth 90% of the actual score.
Exceptions may be granted in emergencies with official proof provided to your lecturer. In order
to take a make-up quiz and exam, please set up a time with your lecturer as soon as possible.
spans.label: LATE

spans.text:
NOTE: Any quiz is considered a “make-up” unless you take the quiz in a class that you register
for with the exception of the fact that your lecturer approves when you submit a letter
that describes the reason for the make-up. In this case, you will be given the actual
score that you get.
spans.label: LATE

spans.text:
Participation

You are required to actively participate in oral practice, questions and answers, role-plays, oral
presentations and other classroom activities. You will be graded on your participation in the class,
not simply on your attendance.
spans.label: CONDUCT

spans.text:
Some of the classes will be conducted with Flipped classroom approach. In this teaching/ learning
approach, students are expected to learn a grammatical concept with video materials outside of the
classroom before class. In the classroom, more time will be spent for communicative activities
such as interactions, discussions and presentations. Thus, it is important to complete homework
before each class time because homework is mainly preparation for the next class
spans.label: ASSIGN

spans.text: • Class participation will be evaluated based on: coming to the class on time, active\nparticipation, and preparedness.\n• Unexcused absences will result in a zero daily participation point.
spans.label: GRADE

spans.text: A distracting behavior such as eating and the unnecessary use of electronic devices (e.g., texting)\nis not allowed.
spans.label: CONDUCT

Important Policies and Resource
Academic Integrity
Every member of the University community is expected to maintain the highest standards of
academic integrity. A student shall not submit work that is falsified or is not the result of the
student's own effort. Infringement of academic integrity by a student subjects that student to
serious penalties, which may include failure on the assignment, failure in the course, suspension
from the University or other sanctions. Please consult Brandeis University Rights and
Responsibilities for all policies and procedures related to academic integrity. Students may be
required to submit work via TurnItIn.com or similar software to verify originality. A student who
is in doubt regarding standards of academic integrity as they apply to a specific course or

assignment should consult the faculty member responsible for that course or assignment before
submitting the work. Allegations of alleged academic dishonesty will be forwarded to the
Department of Student Rights and Community Standards. Citation and research assistance can be
found at Brandeis Library Guides - Citing Sources.
spans.label: INTEGRITY

Success in this course depends heavily on your personal health and well-being. Recognize that stress is
an expected part of the college experience, and it often can be compounded by unexpected setbacks or
life changes outside the classroom. Your other professors and I strongly encourage you to reframe
challenges as unavoidable pathways to success. Reflect on your role in taking care of yourself
throughout the academic year, before the demands of exams and projects reach their peak. Please feel
free to reach out to me about difficulties you may be having that may impact your performance in this
course as soon as it occurs and before it becomes too overwhelming.
